skill.name: priority-judge
skill.description: 优先级判断助手 - 帮用户从混沌的待办事项中判断优先级，确定现在该做什么。当用户说"我有很多事要做"、"帮我理一下"、"排个优先级"、"今天该做什么"时触发。
skill.summary: A decision-support assistant that helps users identify task priorities using a 5-step methodology based on clarity and deadline dimensions.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 9 patterns (7 external_commands, 1 weak crypto, 1 high entropy) but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill is a markdown documentation file containing example dialogue prompts in code blocks - no actual executable code exists. Safe for publication.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive - External Commands
low_findings.description: Static analyzer detected 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' patterns in SKILL.md lines 57-241. These are markdown code blocks containing example dialogue prompts, not actual shell execution code. The skill is purely conversational documentation with no executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Verified file contents - all detected patterns are within markdown documentation code blocks showing example prompts, not actual executable Ruby or shell code.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive - Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at SKILL.md line 3. Line 3 contains only Chinese description text in YAML frontmatter. No cryptographic code exists in this file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only Chinese text describing the skill's purpose. No crypto algorithms present - clear false positive from pattern matcher.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positive - High File Entropy
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'High file entropy (6.94 bits)' suggesting possible binary/encrypted content. The file is a standard UTF-8 encoded markdown document with Chinese characters, which naturally produces higher entropy readings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File is plain text markdown with Chinese UTF-8 encoding. High entropy is expected for non-ASCII text, not indicative of binary or encrypted content.

content.value_statement: Stop feeling overwhelmed by endless to-do lists. This skill uses a proven 5-step decision framework to help you identify the 1-2 most important tasks to focus on right now, based on clarity and deadline dimensions.
actual_capabilities: Collects all your pending tasks through natural conversation
Asks targeted questions about task clarity and deadlines
Applies a 4-quadrant decision matrix to rank priorities
Generates a layered priority list with clear action items
Creates markdown documentation of your priority decisions
Provides ongoing support as you complete tasks
limitations: Requires honest self-assessment about task clarity and deadlines
Best suited for creative/knowledge work, not routine procedural tasks
Does not integrate with external calendar or project management tools
Works best with 5-30 tasks; too few tasks may not need this framework

use_cases.title: Overwhelmed Knowledge Worker
use_cases.description: You have 20+ tasks across multiple projects and feel paralyzed about where to start. Use this skill to quickly identify the 1-2 critical items for today.

use_cases.title: Creative Professional Planning Session
use_cases.description: You work on inspiration-driven projects and struggle with traditional to-do lists. This skill helps you focus on what matters without rigid time-blocking.

use_cases.title: Weekly Priority Reset
use_cases.description: Start each week by dumping all pending tasks and getting a clear priority hierarchy for the week ahead, distinguishing urgent from important.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Priority Check
prompt_templates.prompt: I have several tasks I need to get done. Can you help me figure out what I should focus on first? I'll tell you what's on my plate.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a work session with multiple competing priorities

prompt_templates.title: Weekly Planning Session
prompt_templates.prompt: It's Monday morning and I'm feeling overwhelmed. I have tasks from last week that rolled over, plus new things that came in over the weekend. Can we do a full priority review using your 5-step process?
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginning the week with accumulated backlog

prompt_templates.title: Mid-Day Recalibration
prompt_templates.prompt: I got sidetracked this morning and now it's 2pm. I still have several things I wanted to do today but don't have time for all of them. Help me decide what's still worth doing versus what I should push to tomorrow.
prompt_templates.scenario: Recovering from a derailed morning schedule

prompt_templates.title: Project Overload Recovery
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm juggling 4 different projects and my manager just added another urgent request. I can't do everything at once. Can you help me think through what actually needs to happen today versus what can wait, and create a clear priority document I can reference?
prompt_templates.scenario: Managing competing stakeholder demands across multiple projects

output_examples.input: I need to finish the quarterly report, prepare tomorrow's presentation, respond to 15 emails, update the project timeline, and call the vendor about the delayed shipment.
output: - Call vendor about delayed shipment (impacts project timeline)

output_examples.input: I want to start my own business but I'm not sure where to begin. There's market research, business plan, website, legal setup, funding... it's overwhelming.
output: - Clarify your business concept (you said you haven't thought it through yet)
- You have many tasks but none are 'clear + urgent' yet
- Spend this week defining your business idea before executing any tasks
⏸️ All other tasks wait until concept is clear

best_practices: Be completely honest about whether you've truly thought through a task - unclear tasks waste time when executed prematurely
Always identify the real deadline, not an assumed one - some 'urgent' tasks have no actual deadline
Commit to the 1-2 top priorities before starting work - resist the urge to multitask
anti_patterns: Don't use this for tasks that are already obvious priorities - it's designed for decision paralysis, not simple execution
Avoid inflating deadlines artificially - 'ASAP' is not a deadline, use specific dates
Don't skip the clarity assessment - executing unclear tasks is the #1 productivity killer this skill addresses

faq.question: How is this different from a regular to-do list app?
faq.answer: To-do apps help you track and organize tasks. This skill helps you DECIDE which tasks matter right now. It focuses on the decision-making process, not task storage.

faq.question: Do I need to use this every day?
faq.answer: Use it when you feel overwhelmed or uncertain about priorities. If you already have clarity on what to do, you don't need this skill. It's an on-demand decision tool.

faq.question: What if my priorities change during the day?
faq.answer: That's expected. Come back anytime for a recalibration. The skill helps you reassess based on new information and adjust your focus accordingly.

faq.question: Can I use this for personal tasks or just work?
faq.answer: Absolutely for both. The framework works for any situation where you have multiple competing demands - work projects, household tasks, personal goals, or a mix of all three.

faq.question: What's the 'clarity' dimension about?
faq.answer: Clarity measures whether you have a concrete plan for how to do a task. Many tasks feel overwhelming because you haven't thought through the approach. This skill identifies those tasks and suggests thinking first, executing second.

faq.question: How long does a typical priority session take?
faq.answer: Most sessions take 5-15 minutes depending on how many tasks you have. The goal is quick decision-making, not extensive analysis. You should walk away with clarity, not more confusion.
